title: drynaria neglecta
intro: Drynaria neglecta, also known as Polypodium leucotomos var. neglectum, is a type of fern native to tropical and subtropical regions of Asia, particularly in China, India, and Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Drynaria neglecta include its ability to improve immune function, reduce oxidative stress, and protect against cancer cell growth, as well as its potential to treat conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin diseases. Drynaria neglecta can be prepared into various herbal forms, including decoctions, infusions, and powders, which can be consumed orally or applied topically to the skin for its medicinal benefits.
